candidates.stmt:
Having grown up in Arnos Grove, I’ve seen first-hand how access to green spaces, safe walking routes and a strong local community can shape healthier, happier lives.

Enfield Council needs a strong Green voice to protect and expand these opportunities, because too many residents still face poor air quality, rising living costs and social exclusion. I am standing to tackle the climate crisis, while improving quality of life for everyone in our borough.

I have dedicated all my working life to public service. I am a housing campaigner, and my background is as a legal aid solicitor, as part of which I represented families from the Grenfell Tower fire.



My family live across North Essex. And Harwich was where my grandfather first docked and stayed in the UK when he arrived as a child refugee on the Kindertransport in the 1930s. So this community is very special to me.



After 14 long years this Tory government, supported by our local MP, has crashed the economy, driven up mortgages and broken our NHS. Voters across North Essex are now moving over to Labour. I will be the champion our community needs to ensure our voice is heard loud and clear in parliament.

I have lived in Bowes ward all my life.  I am a secondary school teacher (originally Science, now Health and Social Care) and I have taught locally. I have been a governor at Oakthorpe primary school in the ward since 2003. In addition I volunteer for the NSPCC, working on safeguarding in many local primary schools.  I have been involved in the opposition to the expansion of the Edmonton Incinerator and worked alongside the charity Care for Calais to distribute aid to refugees in Northern France.  I have also been involved in the Enfield Climate Action Forum.
I love working with children and teenagers and I want to help build a better, greener and more sustainable world for their futures. I joined the Green Party as their core values align most closely with my own, those being social justice, environmental protection and Climate justice.

As a resident of Enfield for over 45 years, I have seen this once great borough rapidly decline in all areas of service to its residents in recent years. Our health and indeed wallets have taken a severe pounding.

I, being one of you, want to get inside the council chambers where I can influence change and improve the decisions made in our name.

I hope you entrust me to fulfill my pledge.

Thank you
I am standing because working-class people need a voice against war and the rigged system.

We won’t pay for Trump’s wars!
Fight energy bill poverty 
Enfield council should step in - use hardship funds to bail us out
I will fight for Enfield to expand its support to households from the Crisis and Resilience Fund to bail out those who cannot pay the sky-rocketing energy bills

We fight for:
No cuts to any public services and jobs, and no increases in council tax. Fund the services our community needs and send the bill to Keir Starmer. Re-open libraries and youth services
100% council homes on council land. No more handouts to the property developers. Enfield council to use its existing powers to bring in rent control and freeze social rents and charges. Keep all public land publicly owned.
Enfield council can act now on the cost of living – expand hardship funds so no one is forced into debt, hardship or eviction as big business hikes prices to maximise profits
The working class needs its own party – the trade unions should lead 
Make the super-rich pay – take the wealth and resources off the 1%

The Socialist Party is part of TUSC, co-founding it with former RMT leader Bob Crow and others in 2010.

The Socialist Party offers socialist ideas to build movements against the cost-of-living crisis, NHS privatisation, student fee hikes, library closures, attacks on public services, and against all forms of discrimination and war.

The Socialist Party is fighting for a fundamental change in society. The enormous wealth, the industry, the skills, science and knowledge should be harnessed and planned to meet the needs of all rather than, as it is at the moment, to increase the wealth of a few billionaires.
We have a winning record of building mass working class movements. Our forerunner, Militant, led two mass struggles that won against Margaret Thatcher, seen as the undefeatable ‘Iron Lady’. Our party, including members elected as councillors then, helped lead the mass Liverpool struggle 1984-5 against Thatcher’s cuts programme, winning £60 million for new jobs, c

I am a local Enfieldian of almost 15 years, having grown up in several wards. I am a neuroscience graduate from QMUL and a former RAFAC Cadet Warrant Officer with a passion for colourwork crochet, reading, and ecology.

I joined the Green Party because I am tired of being angry at our local and national government's failures to represent and serve us. I know the Green Party to be a beacon of hope and of our true potential as a nation for all. If elected as a councillor for the Enfield Green Party, I will bring the transparency, dedication, and integrity Enfield desperately needs to steer it safely into the uncertain future.

I am a person of integrity who champions transparency, honesty, and local democracy. A vote for me will bring accountability to our local council.

I am a domestic abuse survivor who is determined to create safe escape and support networks for victims. As a councillor, I will advocate for the ring-fencing of safe houses from future building projects for those escaping domestic abuse, enabling them to stay with their children and pets, and support every initiative that will feasibly protect victims of every background.

I am an outspoken advocate against bigotry and the politics of hate. As a human being, I adamantly oppose the rise of fascism in UK politics and will not allow it to poison the heart of our community. I support the cultural and ethnic diversity of Enfield for the strength and richness it brings to our community. As a councillor candidate and citizen of Enfield, I endeavour to restore our pride in our home. As a councillor, I will advocate for honesty and accountability from our local police services, holding them to account where they fall below standard in serving our communities and keeping us safe.

I am a staunch anti-war candidate who will stand firmly against arms lobbyists, funding sourced from the arms industry, and the narratives of warmongers seeking to divide, exploit, and harm us. I support the boycott, divestment, and

I am an Engineering Graduate and a Senior Project Manager in the Construction Industry, and have 3 children.

I moved to Enfield almost 8 years ago, having worked at the Chase Farm Hospital Re-Development, where I fell in love with the area and thought it was a perfect mixture of close community, amazing history, easy commuting distance to central London and great schools, not to mention the abundance of green space.

Enfield has a lot going for it and I feel it is an unsung gem of North London that needs its profile boosting.
I've noticed the stark contrast in funding and facilities between the areas to the East of the A10 and those to the West and feel Bullsmoor is chronically underfunded, which can be seen by the lack of investment in the parks and communal spaces especially the children's play areas in the Dendridge, Elsinge and Turkey Street Park.

Similarly, the apparent lack of interest of the improving the road safety of Turkey Street and Hertford Road which sees numerous RTA’s and fatalities is astonishing, this will be high on my agenda to address, as well as campaigning for a more frequent service along the Weaver Line, a plan to clear up the rubbish-filled Turkey brook and tackle the lack of investment in sustainable housing developments that actually live up to their ‘Green’ credentials such as delivering the biodiversity net gain, that is a condition for planning permision.

I’m a recent Green Party convert and like the freedom that the party has to genuinely serve the community and look forward to hopefully being able to fulfil my aims after May’s election… see you on May the 7th!
I work in education as a Data Manager

I’ve lived here my whole life and I am studying Politics and International Relations at SOAS, University of London, where I will be graduating this year. I was raised in a family that taught me the values of giving back to my community. I’ve already see the difference that Enfield Labour has made locally and want to build upon those achievements.

As a Labour Councillor, I aim to make Bush Hill Park a safer, cleaner neighbourhood. I am a member of my university’s women’s rugby team and know the importance of having a diverse range of sports programs available to young people, investing into our green spaces and upgrading playground equipment for families. As a flutist,  I am also passionate about organising art and music classes in our community centres, giving residents of all ages the chance to explore creative hobbies. Particularly for young people, I want to run workshops connecting them to companies offering apprenticeships and work experience, alongside public speaking classes to helps build their confidence for the world of work.

With your support, I will echo the voices of residents and tackle the issues that matters most to you. I will work alongside you to make our neighbourhood safer, invest in the next generation and ensure Bush Hill Park is a place that all can proudly call home.

At a time where in Enfield we have a Labour Party who has forgotten its principles and a Conservative Party who offer no more than empty phrases, we need a new way of working for and with the people of Enfield.

There is a level of confrontation between parties on the Council that just fight each other over tired old national issues. We must and will change this, we must work together and improve life for us all in Enfield.

Please take a look at our manifesto on how we will save our Green Belt and Whitewebbs and change how the Council does business at https://tinyurl.com/2p826b2j and if you like what you see vote for change on May 5th and vote Liberal Democrat.

I am originally from South Wales but have lived in England for nearly 20 years and in Enfield for 3 years with my husband and three cats.

I am new to joining a political party and standing as a candidate but I wanted to join the Green Party as a party that is funded and run by people not by the rich.

Unlike Labour, Tories or Reform, the Green Party is run by ordinary working people who can put policies in place that work for ordinary working people.

As an Enfield resident I understand the concerns of the community as they are my concerns too:

- Defend the Green Belt and protect green spaces
- Tackle fly tipping and invest in safer, greener streets
- A cleaner air strategy that sets out our plans for dealing with all sources of air pollution, making our air healthier to breathe, protecting nature and boosting the economy.
- A noise policy that aims to promote good health and quality of life through effective noise management. 
- Support residents through the cost of living crisis , helping those most in need
Making sure that residential flats above shops / restaurants are given recycling bins in line with the new 'Simpler Recycling' scheme.
- Prioritise genuinely affordable housing. Bringing empty homes back into use, and upgrade existing homes to cut bills and improve living standards.
- Stand up for inclusion, promote equality and challenge divisive rhetoric. 
- Improve communication between the Council and residents.

Let's bring real change for Cockfosters. Vote for the Green Party on the 7th May.

I have lived in North London for almost a decade, and more recently in Enfield. Edmonton Green is dear to my heart, as I have family members with close ties to the area.

From being here, I sense that residents in Edmonton Green feel they haven't been heard. I feel the frustration that comes across, as basic needs aren't being taken care of.

Fly-tipping is rife, potholes are everywhere, and yet the price of council tax has gone up. Residents aren't seeing where the extra money they are being asked to pay is going; they don't feel the benefits at all!

I would also like to work with organisations such as the North London Waste Authority to move towards a low-waste North London. This includes reducing the amount we waste as a borough and ensuring that the air around Edmonton Green is not polluted by plastic waste being burned in the Edmonton incinerator. Every resident in Edmonton Green deserves and needs clean air, well maintained green spaces and well funded public services in order to live a decent and healthy life, and I want to fight for the residents of Edmonton Green so that they can have the quality of life they deserve. We're all in this together!

Why Vote ECI?
Voting ECI means choosing people who are part of the community, not separate from it. We are residents, and we are affected by the same issues as everyone else who goes to the ballot box. 
We are not driven by money or personal gain. We believe in equality, and that equality should apply to everyone, not just a specific group or section of the community. We stand with anyone who feels unheard, overlooked, or unable to speak up.
Diversity has helped shape Britain into what it is today, something even reflected in our Brexit 50p coin, which speaks of peace, prosperity and friendship with all nations. That should not just be words, it should be something we live by in our communities.
Many of us are tired of the same politics. We have seen Labour lose touch with its roots, and Conservatives continue to prioritise certain groups over others. While one side makes the cuts and the other asks residents to carry the burden, people like us are left dealing with the consequences.
We are also tired of being divided, of communities being played against each other instead of being brought together. Enfield deserves better. Enfield needs change, and that change starts with us.
We are a party, built by the people, for the people.

I’m out in this area every day and see what’s actually happening — what gets sorted, and what gets left.

Fly-tipping is reported, but in many places it builds up again and again. After a while, people stop reporting it because they don’t expect anything to change.

Part of the problem is practical. If the only option is driving to a distant tip, it’s no surprise people don’t use it — and the rubbish ends up on the street instead.

That affects more than the streets. It changes how people feel about where they live — and over time, people withdraw. You see less connection, more isolation.

I work as a therapist and walk my dogs locally, so I spend a lot of time out in the area day to day. After 25 years working in a university, I also see how younger people are struggling to get a foothold, especially when it comes to housing.

We do need homes — but not just large developments with high service charges. They need to be affordable and properly planned so they work for the area.

I want to see practical recycling options that people can actually use, proper follow-through on local issues, housing that works for local people, and protection of green space.

I live here, I’m out here, and I pay attention.

Enfield Lock should feel safe and looked after.

Enfield has been my home for over 30 years. I am a proud British citizen, a husband, and a father of four children—all of whom were born and raised here. This community has given my family so much, and I feel a deep responsibility to give back.

My journey has not always been easy. Like many who come from abroad, I have experienced struggle, loss, and sacrifice. I live with the pain of being far from my parents, my brother, and my sister. That distance is something I carry every day, and it has shaped who I am—giving me a deeper understanding of hardship, resilience, and the importance of community support.

I studied at Middlesex University, earning a BSc (Hons) in IT and Business Studies, and later completed a Master’s degree in Leadership in the Community at London Metropolitan University. But my real education has come from life—listening to people, helping those in need, and working within our community.
For many years, I have been involved in charitable work, helping to provide food and support to people across Enfield and London who are struggling. I have met families who are doing their best but still finding it hard to get by. These experiences have strengthened my commitment to fairness, dignity, and compassion for everyone.

Coming from a Middle Eastern background, I was raised with values of kindness, generosity, and respect for others. I speak Arabic, Greek, and conversational Turkish, and I have always tried to bring people together, no matter their background. I truly believe that our diversity is our strength.

I am standing with the Green Party because I believe in fairness, equality, and protecting what matters most—our people, our environment, and our future. Green spaces are not just land; they are places where our children play, where families come together, and where our wellbeing is nurtured. I want to help build a healthier, more supportive, and more inclusive Enfield.

I am especially passionate about supporting young people in education, gi

I have lived and worked in Enfield mostly since I chose to move here in 1983, including five years in Enfield Lock. I know what it's like moving house because the rent goes up. My four daughters all grew up in Enfield and have blessed me with six grandchildren. Of course I am concerned about what sort of future they will have. And how they will be able to afford to live.

Social justice and environmental care matter greatly to me. They go hand in hand. I have worked for voluntary groups since 1976 in varied roles. I helped to set up and run Your Environment Shop in Edmonton Green Market Square promoting local Agenda 21 for 11 years, then worked with the Ponders End Community Development Trust for seven years.

We need to create a better quality of life for everyone. I have seen so many changes over the years and not always for the better. So often, residents don't seem to benefit. Decisions are rarely explained clearly. I believe we urgently need councillors who pay attention and listen, who find out what we need instead of following the party line. Green Party councillors vote with their conscience.

Vote for cleaner affordable energy, better energy-efficient housing, less waste and untidiness and phasing out the senseless burning of valuable resources in the Edmonton incinerator.
Vote Green. Help to take better care of ourselves and our environment, our life support system.
Vote for policies which protect us and the environment we live in.
Vote for people who listen to you and make sure your views are heard on the local council.
Please vote for the Green Party on 7th May. Thank you.

I am a devoted family man, embracing the roles of husband, father, and grandfather with joy and deep pride.

Professional Background: 

With a rich history in sales, marketing, and training, I transitioned into education, where my passion lies in championing the potential of Neurodiverse (SEN) learners.

As an educator, behaviour, and intervention specialist, I am dedicated to creating pathways for success and well-being. My expertise extends to life coaching, with a focus on sports and holistic wellness.

Community Engagement: 

My commitment to societal growth is evident in my volunteer work as a community mentor, where I partner with families to navigate life’s challenges.

Vision for Edmonton and Winchmore Hill:

I envision a vibrant, interconnected community where collaboration is the cornerstone. Schools, businesses, faith groups, and residents unite to foster educational excellence, well-being, and meaningful social contributions. I advocate for shared spaces and youth centers that nurture development, supported by a Quid Pro Quo Community Scheme that embodies mutual aid and solidarity.

Integrated Community Care Partnership Programme: 

My dream is to establish a fully integrated community care partnership programme, comprising:

Schools & Colleges

Businesses

Faith Groups

Sports & Athletic Clubs

Gyms & Leisure Centres

Engaged ‘Young at Heart’ over 50s

Proactive Parents

Charitable Organizations

Police

Accessible Community Walk-In Centres

Primary Care Networks

This initiative is designed to weave a tapestry of support, empowering every segment of our society.

Political Stance:

I stand for pragmatic politics, balancing idealism with realism, and recognising the merit in diverse perspectives. I am an advocate for integrity in political service, believing that true representation intertwines the interests of leaders with those of their constituents.

Advocacy and Belief:

A community’s strength is measured by the support it offers its most vulnerable

I would like to start by introducing myself. I am no stranger to public service having served on Enfield Council since I was first elected in May 2018. I was born in Chase Farm hospital and attended St Andrew’s Primary School in Churchbury Lane and Bishop Stopford’s Secondary School in Brick Lane. I chose Enfield as my home and am proud to live in the town I was born and grew up in. In my spare time I volunteer as a Cub Scout Leader with the 4th Enfield (St Andrew’s) Scout Group.

I am passionate about Enfield and have spoken out as a Local Councillor against our local Labour led Council and the appalling way they choose to run the borough. In Enfield, sadly, we are all paying significantly more in Council Tax for less services, such as the removal of our weekly bin collections. I am seeking to represent the Enfield North constituency in Parliament where if elected I will champion Enfield and be a strong voice in Westminster for the issues that affect Enfield.

This election we face clear choice between the safety and security offered by our Prime Minister, Rishi Sunak, or the uncertainty that a government led by Sir Keir Starmer would undoubtedly bring. Since the last election the Conservative Government has led the Country through three significant shocks. Firstly, we were hit by a worldwide pandemic, as if that wasn’t enough, we then had the illegal invasion of the Ukraine by Putin which caused havoc with energy prices and caused significant inflation across the western world. Finally, we have had conflict in the middle east which sadly continues.

Throughout the Pandemic the government worked hard to protect our economy through the development of the vaccine and the furlough scheme. These kinds of economic shocks are unusual, and we have had three in a relatively short space of time. This makes it all the more remarkable that the government has worked hard to bring inflation under control which is now back where it should be. This has meant that the government i

I have lived in Enfield my whole life, and I care deeply about the communities and green spaces that make this borough what it is.
I studied locally at Capel Manor College and have spent years learning, volunteering and working in local green spaces, including Forty Hall Farm, Jubilee Park, and Trent Park. Growing up in Enfield, I’ve seen how important our parks, woodlands and community spaces are, and how they need to be protected and better supported.
I now work as an environmental educator with a wildlife charity, supporting children, families and communities to connect with nature. While my work takes me across London, I see every day how access to green space, clean air and safe outdoor environments shapes people’s health, wellbeing and opportunities.
I joined the Green Party because I believe that caring for people and caring for nature go hand in hand. When we invest in our green spaces and our communities together, we create a fairer, healthier borough for everyone.

I’ve lived in Enfield for nearly 40 years. My children and grandchildren live in the borough and attend local schools. 
I joined the Green Party in 2009 because of my increasing concerns about climate change and biodiversity loss. I believed, and still believe, that they can only be tackled through a complete transformation of society and the economy. And this transformation is also needed to address the problems of violence and inequality.
I know from campaigning in previous years that many Enfield residents share these views. In the past that’s not been enough. But now the growth of the Green Party and recent election results show that many people are eager to vote for the change they have long wanted. 
If elected I will work to:
•	Embed the protection of our green spaces, Green Belt and climate in all council decisions.
•	Oppose all attempts to scapegoat minority communities
•	Increase council and community scrutiny of the plans and actions of local government
•	Improve local services.

At a time where in Enfield we have a Labour Party who has forgotten its principles and a Conservative Party who offer no more than empty phrases, we need a new way of working for and with the people of Enfield.

There is a level of confrontation between parties on the Council that just fight each other over tired old national issues. We must and will change this, we must work together and improve life for us all in Enfield.

Please take a look at our manifesto at https://tinyurl.com/5n7smsxs and if you like what you see vote for change on May 5th and vote Liberal Democrat.

Because Enfield’s problems are both national and local, I oppose austerity and local council budget cuts as well as the privatisation of our public services. Seeing the destructive consequences of these policies and of the cost of living crisis on local working class communities, increasing local welfare support and access to services is therefore a priority for me. Local people need more housing, disability, food and energy costs, afterschool, social and Council Tax support!

I stand against scapegoating of working class communities and migrants – I will fight to uphold the unity of people and defend their rights to access improved social support!

I have been a resident of Enfield since 2001, am a Politics, Philosophy and History graduate and have two decades of community organising experience. As a local resident, I have lived experience of the countless issues currently impacting the local living standards which the people of Enfield face.

With strong community, I received 866 votes in the 2022 local elections as an Independent, providing a strong community challenge to the mainstream parties. As residents, we now have a chance of building on this and making a difference as the Enfield Community Independents.

I moved to the UK as a teenager to complete my studies here. My whole world widened as I moved to place as diverse and full of life as London. Wanting to learn more and challenge myself, I have completed a Master’s degree at the London School of Economics in Social Anthropology during Covid. I now work in recruitment at an international company, talking to new people every day. I have moved out from my parents’ house and into Enfield two years ago, and I love the neighbourhood. I would like to start a family here, and live in a place where I am excited to raise kids and call home. I care deeply about this community and I would like to get to know as many of my neighbours as I can.
Why am I standing?
I cannot stand by as the UK government supports genocide abroad and erodes hard fought democratic and human rights at home. I cannot stand by as ordinary people get poorer while billionaires not only enrich themselves at everybody else’s expense, but also bankroll the rise of fascism and the fossil fuel industry that destroy the very Earth we live on. If I don’t do everything I can do now, there will be no future for me, my kids or anyone else. 
Why is it important to vote ECI?
ECI is the only local group that care about the area and it’s made up of ordinary citizens who know we deserve better. This is a pivotal moment in UK electoral history, when for the first time in a century both Labour and the Tories are on the back-foot. It is a once in a lifetime opportunity to change the status quo and reverse decades of decline in social services and social housing. It is time to reverse the rise of hateful rhetoric and wealth inequality. People voted for change after 14 years of Tories and did not get it. Labour is only delivering the same. It is time for real change!

I’m standing as your Green Party candidate because I believe politics should start with people — not promises.

I grew up in North London and come from a Kurdish background. This community shaped who I am, and I’m committed to giving back to it.

For nearly ten years, I’ve worked in mental health, supporting people in real crisis — not from behind a desk, but face to face. I’ve seen what happens when services are stretched and when communities are overlooked.

That’s why I’m standing.

I will fight for safer streets, better mental health support, and a greener, healthier environment for all.

Most of all, I want every child and young person in Enfield to grow up feeling safe, supported, and valued.

This isn’t about politics as usual — it’s about real care, real action, and a community that no one is left behind from.

Vote Green on 7th May.

I have lived  Locally  for over six years. I earned an IT Diploma at Enfield College of Haringey and later studied law at the University of Law. My time volunteering with youth at Albany Church Restore Community Youth Club inspired my commitment to community building.

"If elected, I will focus on three priorities: better access to social services, improved local infrastructure, and safer roads and transport. My goal is a Jubilee that works for everyone—especially our elderly residents and children."

I have lived in the Enfield since 2019 and within our community we all look forward to actual inclusive accountability rather than the facade of democracy limiting our influence.
We Need:
- More building of industrial & business units
- Tighter rent controls
- Increased youth service facilities

We Don't Need
- Lack of transparency for decisions taken
- Preventing council borrowing for local projects
- Private sector control of house building

I have lived in Enfield for over 24 years. I grew up here, and I’ve seen first-hand how much our town has changed. Enfield was once a place we were proud to call home — clean, safe, and full of community spirit. But over time, things have slipped. We’re seeing more potholes, more dumped rubbish, and a feeling that the area is being neglected.
Like many residents, I’ve waited for things to improve. But I’ve come to realise that change won’t happen unless people step forward and take responsibility. That’s why I’m putting myself forward to become a councillor — not to talk, but to act.
I want to restore pride in Enfield. To make it a place where people feel heard, where our streets are cared for, and where the community comes first. I believe in bringing people together-because when we stand as one town, one community, we are stronger.
Together, we can build an Enfield that works for everyone — united, fair, and full of opportunity.

Lower Edmonton deserves people who fight for us. I’ve spent months speaking to many of you, and while I’m not a councillor, I pushed your councillors to get Bounces Road resurfaced because you asked. I came to the UK aged 6 and went to Edmonton County School and Woodhouse College. I have a strong background working in this community, first at 18, running Kate Osamor MP’s campaign to have her elected and then a youth justice worker at St Peter’s Church. We’re making progress, reversing brutal Tory cuts that hit our community hard. I know families here want action to cut the cost of living, build more council homes and more to improve existing ones, clean up our streets, enforce against rogue HMOs, and better parking management. We have great trees in Edmonton, but we must prune them regularly. With £20 million secured from the Labour government, this council election is our chance to restore pride in Lower Edmonton.

I’ve lived in the Ward for 25 years now, and it’s a really lovely neighbourhood. That despite the general neglect that comes with being on the edge of the Borough. Everything comes last to New Southgate, if at all. We need affordable and secure housing, but recent experience with the Council planning process shows just how quick they are to push inappropriate development designed for private gain. The flytipping and general rubbish make the streets feel uncared for and unsafe, and safety for women and girls is a passion of mine. And the pollution from the A406 is a threat to all our health.

The Council desperately needs Green voices to scrutinise decision-making, and make sure it is responsive to a more diverse Enfield. It’s been in the hands of Labour and the Tories for too long.

Vote for a Green future.

Building houses in Trent Park, on Vicarage Farm and in Crews Hill will despoil our precious Green Belt while failing to provide the types of affordable housing that is desperately needed. Green councillors will push for a housebuilding policy that puts the council firmly in the driving seat, building new housing quickly and in locations and ways that are environmentally sustainable and will not add further traffic problems. Our focus will be on the wellbeing of people and planet, not on developers' bottom line.

I’ve lived in and around Oakwood for over a decade, and it’s where I'm raising my young family. My children attend nursery and primary school locally, and we often spend weekends in Oakwood Park. Like many families here, I care deeply about keeping our area safe, green, and supportive of local communities and businesses.

As a working mum in professional services, I solve problems every day to help people and organisations perform at their best. Locally, I see where the system isn’t working as it should. Neighbours and residents tell me about burglary and car theft, missed bin collections, dangerous potholes, overgrown verges, and how hard it can be to get a timely response from the Council when something goes wrong. These are everyday issues which affect our daily lives.

I’m standing for the Green Party because I believe that we need a fairer, more community-focused approach in Oakwood and across the Enfield borough. As your councillor, I will focus on practical action: working with residents and police to improve safety, holding the council to account on basic services, improving road safety and travel connections, protecting and enhancing our green spaces, opposing the proposed 'new town' developments impacting Crews Hill and Vicarage Farm, and ensuring local people have a real voice in planning decisions.

Most importantly, I will be visible, responsive, and accountable, because I believe that local councillors should work for the communities and local residents that they serve.

Oakwood is a wonderful place to live. Vote Green on 7th May to put our community first and protect what matters the most.

I have lived in and around Enfield Borough  since 1964. I went too school at Raglan and  Edmonton County. i have stood for Enfield Liberal Democrats in local elections since 1994. I've also served on the executive of Enfield Liberal Democrats since 1994 as chair and  other executive Committee positions.  Im Jewish i was married at southgate progressive synagogue. I've run a fashion business and i was a full time carer for my ex partner until i retired last year. I've always stood up for people less fortunate than myself. I became  politically  active too help others from the experiences ive had through my life. Im also a Triple Heart Bypass survivor. I play golf love football walks and an avid traveller as i love too experience different  cultures and expand my knowledge of them. I feel i have lived a life that has had enough experience too serve others too the best of my ability which i will do if i'm selected  as your councillor.

I'm standing as your Green Candidate for a healthier and happier Palmers Green. I've lived here for over 10 years with my family,.
 
I believe change is something we all need to do; it's not something that someone else will do for us. 
 
Enfield Council needs to listen and involve the community in the future we all need.
 
Being in nature makes us healthier (and it's fun), we really need our only life support system to survive. I will help Broomfield Park and Hazelwood Rec become even better places to play, explore, and feel happy.  
 
Did you know that children get worse exam results when air pollution is high? We need the air to be cleaner so it’s easier for us all to breathe. 
 
We need to use the 4000 unused homes instead of levelling Crews Hill garden centres for a new town on the green belt.
 
I look forward to hearing more about your views.

As a chef who has worked in local, family owned restaurants, I know the benefits hospitality provides to local vibrant town centres,  they are places to meet and share stories, creating jobs and opportunities in the local area.

Gambling dens on the other hand are on the rise in Enfield and Palmers Green. Rather than a social benefit, the harm caused by gambling is well known. If elected I will resist any attempts to allow for more adult gaming shops on our high street and bring transparency and openness to local politics and planning decisions.

The Green Party stands for the protection and improvement of green spaces and fairer housing developments that put social, economic and environmental justice at the centre of decision making.

A vote for the Green Party is a vote for hope, equality and is a vote to show that the Greens are the new and only party of the left in Britain.

I have lived and worked in Enfield for many years, serving our community through healthcare and local business. Every day, I meet residents who are struggling — whether it’s accessing NHS services, dealing with poor housing conditions, or simply trying to be heard by the Council.

Ponders End is a strong and diverse community, but too many people feel forgotten. Basic issues like street cleanliness, fly-tipping, and slow repairs are not being taken seriously. Residents in council housing often feel they have no voice — and that must change.

As a healthcare professional, I see first-hand the impact of inequality, pollution, and poor living conditions on people’s health. Cleaner air, safer streets, and better local services are not luxuries — they are essential.

I am standing for the Green Party because we believe in fairness, transparency, and putting people before profit. I will work to:

Improve communication between the Council and residents
Tackle fly-tipping and keep our streets clean
Push for better housing standards and faster repairs
Support safer streets for families, women, and young people
Strengthen local NHS and community services
Most importantly, I will listen. This community deserves a councillor who is present, accessible, and truly on your side.

Let’s bring real change to Ponders End — together.

Hi I'm Robert Lived in Ponders End since 1976 but family goes back to 1939.

I have been active in the Community since the 1980s but became more involved around 2005 with football and futsal in Ponders End Park and then working with the Ponders End Community Development Trust.

Community Games started in 2012 and food bank, food projects have followed working with St James Church and Community Smiles as well as the development Trust

Rob has been Chairman for the Friends of Durants and Ponders End parks for the past 3 years, working to make the parks a better place for the community

I am Single, live in Ponders End and work as an after school play leader

I have lived in Enfield for nearly 20 years and am married with three small children.

I am passionate about our community, I work and volunteer to make Enfield the best place it can be. I care passionately about the borough and want Enfield to get a beter deal from the council.  

I will fight to protect Enfield’s Green Belt from being built on. I oppose Enfield Labour’s plans to build thousands of properties on our green space.

I will ensure that Ridgeway Ward is not neglected and that fly-tipping is removed quickly. I want Enfield to be a place everyone can enjoy.

I will also oppose the proliferation of massive tower blocks in Enfield. I will fight to preserve the suburban nature of our borough.

I believe that votes should be earned & not taken for granted. If you vote for me I promise to listen and work hard to represent all residents in Enfield

I’m a Ridgeway Ward resident and mum, and I’m standing for the Green Party because I care about keeping this a safe and welcoming place to live and raise a family.

I’ve worked for Enfield Council and other London boroughs in areas including public health, community safety and child protection. I understand how the system works and how decisions are made.

Living here, I’ve seen how quickly the area is changing, with new developments but not always the infrastructure to match. I want to see safer roads around schools, proper investment in the community, and more transparency about where developer money is going.

I’m also committed to protecting the Green Belt and the character of the area, so that growth is balanced and doesn’t come at the expense of the spaces people value.

I’m standing as a candidate for the Green Party in May because I believe Enfield deserves a fresh, community-led approach to local politics. Only a Green council will deliver that.

I moved to Enfield 27 years ago, raised my family here, and I’ve watched successive council cabinets lose touch with the people they serve. Decisions without consultation. Cuts to services. Missed targets on housing and net zero. And now the loss of green belt and park land spaces in Crews Hill and Whitewebbs.

Enfield deserves better. A council that works for you. That protects your green spaces. That prioritises affordable housing, safer streets, air quality, vital public services, active transport AND its climate commitments – because none of these things are optional. A council that is open, transparent and accountable.

If elected as your local councillor for Ridgeway, I’ll do everything I can to make that happen.

Arben Neza is 45 years old and lives in Edmonton with his partner, where they are bringing up their young family.

He has run his own successful construction company, in Edmonton, for over 16 years. He attended London Metropolitan University obtaining a degree in International relations and British politics. Arben is active in his local community and is driven by a desire to help and support others and to build and safeguard a better future for all.

I moved to the UK from Bangladesh as a student in 2013. Over the last 12+ years, I have studied and worked in South Wales, Bath, and Glasgow. Eventually, I settled in North London, charmed by its diversity, social cohesion, and activist spirit. Not too long ago, I proudly took my citizenship oath at Enfield Civic Centre.

Having grown up in a close-knit family, I am passionate about building community. I believe that community transcends simply putting homes next to each other. It requires social spaces, events, and amenities. This is why I oppose the submitted plan for Southbury Leisure Park. Building housing for the sake of housing while ignoring residents' concerns breaks community spirit. The proposed redevelopment would remove valuable spaces such as the cinema and restaurants, catering only to those who can afford to rent in the sprawling towers.

This is also a wasted opportunity to build affordable homes. At a time when the cost of living is high and temporary accommodations are on the rise, contributing to an overall housing crisis, Build to Rent properties in tower blocks is unsuitable and only serves Labour's Westminster agenda.

As councillor, I will be the voice of Southbury's residents, and push for a considerate urban design that ensures affordable housing, green spaces, sufficient parking provision, and amenities for the neighbourhood.

With a third of its residents born outside the UK, Southbury is a diverse ward, comprising people from all races, faiths, and ethnicities. I have served as Equality Officer in various groups, and am passionate about interfaith, intersectional activism. To further my efforts to eliminate social isolation, I have also signed the pledge to extend free public transport to all. While tackling climate change and air pollution, this will put people first and ensure equal access to mobility.

I have been involved in social justice and environmental issues since university. I spent two years in the Board of Directors for Peopl

I came to North London from Italy eleven years ago, and like many neighbours here, I chose to put down roots. Over 40% of Enfield residents were born outside the UK: this borough's diversity is one of its greatest strengths, and I'm proud of being a part of it.

I've spent almost two decades in grassroots activism and community organising, alongside a career in software engineering. I believe in solving problems from the ground up: working with communities, not around them. The Southbury Leisure Park development is the defining local issue right now; as councillor, I would propose an alternative scheme prioritising affordable housing, green spaces and community needs, with residents involved in shaping it from the start.

Economic wellbeing, social justice and environmental health are inseparable. Southbury deserves councillors who act on that, and I'd be honoured to be one of them.

I’m Katie - a doctor and a mum, who works in the borough and has lived in north London for over 15 years. I joined the Greens because I believe that politicians have lost touch with what is really affecting peoples’ lives - and I see the effects of poverty, air pollution and inequality in my daily work.

I ran for the Greater London Assembly for Enfield and Haringey in 2024, coming third with over 26,000 votes. I’ve held a national role with my medical Royal College in their climate work, and have worked with organisations including Enfield Climate Action Forum (EnCaf) and Mums for Lungs to highlight the importance of green spaces and clean air in our borough.

I believe that doing the right thing for our planet will improve lives and livelihoods - this May, it’s time to vote Green for the future of Southgate and London.

As a Southgate resident for over 30 years, and as your councillor from 2018-2022, I truly understand the issues facing our area. My background as a director of a global engineering company means I am well placed to ensure that the council is careful with your money - while on council, I held Enfield council to account on their Meridian Water project and on the Enfield Regeneration Scheme, highlighting serious shortcomings. I am committed to economic, social and environmental justice, and I would be honoured to receive your vote to rejoin Enfield Council.

I was born and raised in Enfield and I am committed to representing our local residents with transparency and clarity.

I am standing as a candidate in Southgate because I want to give our residents a voice and an opportunity to have a say in the decision making of our ward. I want to champion growth opportunities for young people, ensure our elderly are supported, and to ensure a brighter future for everyone living in Enfield.

My academic interests lie in sustainable development and green growth, and since I’ve graduated, I have been working in public affairs and policy research, supporting government departments, diplomatic missions, and the charity sector.

I have also campaigned for increased accessibility to higher education for marginalised communities, and advocated for young people’s mental wellbeing post COVID-19. Currently, I am launching a community led platform to tackle the student rental crisis.

I want to see fairer, community centered solutions to the problems in our borough, to ensure that Southgate is a place we’re proud to call home!

I’m standing for the Green Party in Town Ward because I believe protecting our environment and supporting the most vulnerable in our society should go hand in hand.

I’ve lived in the borough for the past 12 years, first in Edmonton and now in Enfield Town. I joined the Greens because I believe environmental protection and social justice are inseparable, and because Labour no longer acts that way, particularly on the green belt, and on support for the most vulnerable in our society.

I work in green technology, helping businesses and households transition to renewable energy sources and making electric vehicles more affordable to run.

I want to see Town Ward’s green spaces genuinely looked after. The New River is a much-loved local waterway on our doorstep, yet poor maintenance has significantly impacted its fish population and wider biodiversity. This is exactly the kind of issue a Green councillor would demand accountability on.

I want to be a councillor who shows up, listens, and pushes for better.

I have lived in Enfield for over 20 years, and raised my 4 children here, most of that time in Town Ward. I would love to represent Enfield people and support the facilities in this area as a Councillor. I work as a Managing Partner with an advertising agency.

I am passionate about supporting people equally and fairly, regardless of their experience and background. I have been a member of the Green Party for over 6 years. The Green party are now the most popular party in London (YouGov).

I am willing to work hard in the local area. I have volunteered as a governor at George Spicer school for 8 years, chairing the board for most of that time, through their expansion to double their size

At a time where in Enfield we have a Labour Party who has forgotten its principles and a Conservative Party who offer no more than empty phrases, we need a new way of working for and with the people of Enfield.

There is a level of confrontation between parties on the Council that just fight each other over tired old national issues. We must and will change this, we must work together and improve life for us all in Enfield.

Please take a look at our manifesto on how we will save our Green Belt and Whitewebbs and change how the Council does business at https://www.enfieldlibdems.org.uk/lib-dem-manifesto-2026j and if you like what you see vote for change on May 7th and vote Liberal Democrat.

Enfield has been home to me and my family for the last 12 years. During this time, I have been actively involved in the community and have become increasingly aware of the issues facing Southgate & Wood Green residents, including the steady decline in public and social services and of the local area itself, and the resulting strain on people’s everyday lives. The pandemic and cost-of-living crisis served to highlight these issues even further, demonstrating the vast inequalities within the borough. 

In 2022, I stood as a Liberal Democrat council candidate, campaigning against the ill-thought-through LTNs imposed on Southgate by Labour-run Enfield Council: as well as fighting to protect our local greenbelt, enhance essential green spaces, and regenerate our local high streets. Conversations on the doorsteps revealed a strong desire for political change, a shift away from two-party politics, and despair at the declining state of public services. Many people felt hopeless; grappling with a cost-of-living crisis that forces them to focus on day-to-day survival rather than striving for a better future for themselves, their families, and communities.

The Liberal Democrats offer sensible, forward-thinking solutions to these fundamental issues. Our commitment to fairness and equality can help bridge the gaps within our community, empowering individuals to aspire to a better future. By addressing these critical concerns, the Liberal Democrats will work to restore faith in the political system and inspire a renewed sense of possibility for Southgate and Wood Green residents.

I was born and raised in North London and have been living in Enfield for the last few years. I love being a part of this community. As a parent and a children’s book editor I am constantly inspired to make a better world for future generations. If elected I would use the skills and knowledge I have from years as a trade union representative. I believe in working together, using our power as a community to make change, and I know how to negotiate to get the best outcome for the people I represent. There are so many areas where a Green council would improve lives - from healthcare to housing and education to climate action. I’m particularly passionate about opposing austerity in local government as well as divesting from fossil fuels and companies complicit in genocide and have therefore signed the Greens Organise pledge against austerity and the PSC pledge for Palestine. I would be a voice for the people of Upper Edmonton - making sure the council is held accountable, that we are honest and transparent and making decisions with you not for you.

I am a 21 year old university student standing to push back against cuts in our area to services which affect working families, vulnerable residents and young people. We need councillors with energy to achieve the real change which our community needs.

The residents of Enﬁeld deserve elected oﬃcials who will put them ﬁrst, above party or any other aﬃliation.

The upcoming ballot provides the ﬁrst opportunity for voters in Enﬁeld to pass judgement on Keir Starmer. With many in the Borough impacted by the two child beneﬁt cap and the means testing of the winter fuel allowance for pensioners;  this will be a real test for Labour.

The Labour run council also has its own problems with ongoing dissatisfaction with housing provision as well as overall cuts to services. Enﬁeld Council's recent proposal to shutter several libraries is an example of the widespread  consternation that is currently being experienced by residents all across the Borough.

For the ﬁrst time in Enﬁeld, voters have a real chance to elect a local resident, independent of the mainstream parties, to represent them.

On election day, please use this opportunity to vote and ensure your voice is heard.

I’m Toby and I live in Meridian Water. I previously worked as an actor, having a small role on Game of Thrones and receiving critical acclaim in London’s West End. I now work in construction and live in Meridian Water with my wife who is expecting our first child. 
I am a retired member of the Royal Navy Reserve, having submitted my resignation in response to the UK’s role in the Palestinian genocide. I studied B/A International Relations and Politics joint Major at Sussex University and M/A Acting at the University of Essex. I was born in and have lived in London most of my life.
Why am I standing?
I am invested in the future of Enfield as a community for myself and my new family
to grow in. With my knowledge of construction I can see first-hand the failings in the Meridian One council-led project which is years behind schedule with spiraling costs. The council is almost 1.5 billion pounds in debt and risks bankruptcy.
Fly tipping is out of control, there’s potholes everywhere, schools are closing and pollution and dust from waste centers is making residents’ lives a misery and endangering the health of our children. I believe the council has a fantastic opportunity to make Enfield an even better place to live for all of our rich and beautifully diverse community. I believe in Enfield, and I’m standing to hold the council, and be held by you, the residents, to account, because that’s what we all deserve. 
A council that works for us, for all of Enfield.
The council has been dominated by Labour and Conservatives for too long. The Tories are making pledges they can’t afford to keep, even saying they’ll tear up cycle lanes put in by a Conservative government, not even realising this could cost us, the taxpayers, 20 million pounds. 
Labour is dominated by careerists who put party politics above the needs of us, the residents. Reform simply do not represent our fantastically rich and diverse mix of cultures and ethnicities, and they want to sell off our treasured NHS. I

Originally from North London, I have been a resident in the Whitewebbs ward of Enfield for over 15 years. I raised my family here, with both my children going to Lavender Primary and then Chase Community school before they went on to study at University.

I work as a Site Facilities Manager at Oasis Academy Hadley, which is a Nursery, Primary and Secondary School in Enfield.

I am the Academy Eco-Champion and, as well as putting Sustainability at the heart of my Facilities Management role, I also lead the Academy Eco-committee made up of staff and students.

I have over 30 years of experience managing buildings and projects in cultural, creative and education venues in London. In these roles I have a history of working with the community stakeholders in listening, communicating and getting things done.

I joined the Green Party in April 2025 as I was inspired by their progressive policies and commitment to building communities and a fairer, society for everyone, now and in the future.

I am committed to being a willing, open minded and compassionate councillor who will honestly represent the residents of Whitewebbs ward to the best of my ability in order to improve their quality of life.

As a member of the Guardians of Whitewebbs I am strongly opposed to Enfield councils plan to sell off Whitewebbs Park.

Hope for a better, greener, fairer future and pragmatic plans to achieve it. That's why I joined the Green Party in 2021, the same year I moved to Enfield.

Enfield has so much going for it - beautiful green spaces, vibrant diverse communities, high streets with so much unrealised potential. 
We need decent, affordable housing, clean streets and lovely places to spend time together as friends and neighbours. 
We need our Council to keep our precious green spaces like Whitewebbs Park for our kids and their kids, not sell if off to corporations who'll never give it back.

But to achieve this, we need councillors who aren't steeped in parties of austerity, division and corporate interests. 
Having Greens in a room where decisions are made is a game changer. 
If I get elected to be one of those Greens, I'll work tirelessly for everyone who needs help and for the local communities I care deeply about.

I've lived in Enfield for almost 5 years now and I love it. But recently I've become very worried by decisions, like the Whitewebbs and Crews Hill developments, that are being made with little concern for locals or the environment. I'm standing as a Green councillor because this is the only party that is honestly talking about solving the twin crises of inequality and climate change. Together we can build a better Enfield.

I joined the Liberal Democrats because I’m fed up with a political culture that is more interested in seeking someone to blame than finding solutions. People are sick of politics not because they don’t want change, but because they have lost faith in the ability of the system to deliver. I’ve lived in Enfield for over a decade, and in that time I’ve seen the many problems that afflict our borough, and I’ve been disappointed at how little progress there is. Some areas (such as our hollowed-out high streets and our overwhelmed health service) have even moved backwards. It’s time for a fresh approach. Edmonton and Winchmore Hill are also affected by the same national and global issues that affect the rest of the UK. Social care will require creative and positive solutions to cope with an ageing population, and the climate crisis urgently needs action. In all these areas, I believe the right way to make progress is to gather motivated people from across our local communities and faithfully represent their interests to the national government. I believe an MP to represent Edmonton and Winchmore Hill will need two things: the empathy to treat people as individuals and understand their needs, and the analytical skill to connect policy to deliver real change. My experience as a volunteer worker in emotional support and tutoring roles, combined with my professional career as a software engineer gives me what I think is the right combination.

I’ve lived in Enfield for nearly three years and in my time here, have connected with many wonderful members of our community. I am deeply passionate about social justice, having worked in the charity sector around prevention education to tackle harmful behaviours such as discrimination, harassment and sexual violence.

I’m standing for the Green Party because Enfield deserves practical, community‑focused leadership. I want to support local businesses, tackle fly‑tipping, push for genuinely affordable housing, and bring real accountability to council decisions. I support ethical investment, ensuring so public money is directed towards sustainable, responsible, community-focussed projects that works for residents.

I first got involved in politics as a student, and having been elected as President of my Students’ Union. I’m standing because I believe Enfield can be fairer, greener, and more hopeful; a borough where decisions are made with people.

Vote Green on 7th May.

The Green Party is the only party which will seriously address the gaping inequalities in society. It is ridiculous that some people can amass fortunes of many billion pounds, while so many of us struggle merely to get by, The basic system of our society is set up almost entirely for the benefit of the multi-millionaires (and billionaires) and it has to change.

It is also a system set up to ensure that we do not tackle climate change in any meaningful manner. That is a disaster waiting to happen - and it won't have to wait very long. Any party not fully committed to net zero is not on your side - they are on the side of profit and big business. The human race is out of kilter with nature, dooming far too many species to extinction.

And increasingly we are out of kilter with each other too. Wars are in no-one's interest apart from the power-hungry. Our country should not be involved, and should unhesitatingly follow international law - something they have signally failed to do.

Serial entrepreneur and community advocate with a proven track record in government digital transformation. Mark helps public sector organisations streamline operations and reduce costs by up to 85% without compromising citizen outcomes. I am standing to deliver:

Better parking for local residents
Safer streets for our community
Action on overdevelopment and HMOs

A stronger local voice for Winchmore Hill.

My name is Matt McLaren and I’m asking for your vote to represent Winchmore Hill on Enfield Council in the election on 5th May.

Our votes can change this failing Labour Council without endorsing the Conservatives and their appalling misconduct at the heart of Government or their plans to bulldoze the Green Belt. Enfield Labour’s recent Local Plan and the Conservative Governments proposed ‘Developer’s Charter’ would give the green light to developers on this.

I live on Green Lanes, between Meadowcroft Road and Eaton Park Road, and I'm standing to represent Winchmore Hill because I want local people to have a Councillor they can actually trust to have the courage of their convictions, just like Cllr Dinah Barry who’s represented Winchmore Hill on the Council for the last 8 years.

When Dinah saw first-hand the failings of Enfield Labour after a new Council Leader was installed, she took the hugely courageous step of calling out the dictatorial attitude and misconduct of that regime – stepping away from the party she loved and forming a new independent group on the Council, Community First. This was true leadership which demonstrated real integrity. I absolutely applaud both Dinah herself and all her Community First colleagues for their bravery and sacrifice in doing this.

The Community First group, which Dinah herself leads, now includes Liberal Democrat and Green Councillors. It is continuing to show real leadership – such as their recent call in of Enfield Labour’s decision to make the Fox Lane LTN permanent.

We applaud the approach of Community First, with Lib Dem Cllr Ayfer Orhan working constructively with Green and independent colleagues. As such Enfield Liberal Democrats have formed a partnership with local independents for the upcoming elections – Together for Enfield – working with popular local independents to stand on a clear platform of restoring local democracy and empowering local residents.

Dinah has been enormously supportive - both of my mission

After many years in local politics, almost 20 years as a local Councillor and 8 years as a Cabinet member of schools and children services, I am excited about a collective return to an old-fashioned idea of politicians working for and listening to local people, the voluntary sector and businesses. A collaborative way of working together for the betterment of Enfield as a whole, heralds a genuinely exciting and new way of representing residents in the best tradition of a co-operative movement.

Please take a look at our manifesto on how we will save our Green Belt and Whitewebbs and change how the Council does business at https://tinyurl.com/2p826b2j and if you like what you see vote for change on May 5th and vote Liberal Democrat.
